Sport

Topic:Olympic Games

Elena Lilik from Germany, Jessica Fox from Australia and Viktoria Wolffhardt from Austria during the cross event.(Getty Images: Sebastian Kahnert/picture alliance)

Jessica Fox's quest for an historic third Olympic medal in Paris is over with her sister Noemie eliminating the superstar in their kayak cross round.

Afterwinning the C1 and K1 titles, Fox was attempting a golden treble with the new Olympic event added to the Paris schedule.

But her plans came unstuck on Sunday night (AEST) when a costly gate error meant she failed to make the top two to advance to the quarter-finals, finishing last.

"I just didn't pull out my best race," said the 30-year-old, who owns six Olympic medals, including three gold from her four Games.

"It was tough off the start, I got really tangled, and I was trying to chase.

"I turned fourth, and climbed up one spot, and almost pulled off the overtake at the last gate.

"That's kayak cross, and you can have a plan, but nothing goes to plan.

Noemie Fox leads the field during a kayak cross heat. Noemie is still in with a shot of a medal.(Getty Images: Justin Setterfield)

"I'm gutted, but at the same time, when you see your little sister (Noemie) win the heat, I was really proud of her."

The Fox siblings were drawn in the same four-paddler heat, against veteran Spaniard and three-time Olympic medallist Maialen Chourraut, and Dutch paddler Martina Wegman.

Noemie, 27, got off to a flying start, storming clear with her sister at the back of the field.

Jessica went past Wegman but as she attempted to claw ahead of Chourraut she missed a gate and had to paddle back around which ruled her out of the running.

After crossing the line the elder Fox immediately embraced Noemie, who is making her Olympics debut.

Earlier, Tim Anderson won his heat to book his quarter-final berth in the men's event while Tristan Carter also advanced after finishing second.

Jessica said while there was disappointed there was also relief after wrapping up a monster campaign in France, that also included being one of Australia's flag-bearers at the opening ceremony.

"There's also some relief in finishing," she said.

"There's partly disappointment to not progress and to not try and have a crack at that third medal but there's pride, relief and exhaustion from the last week of competing."

AAP

title: Jessica Fox misses out on claiming third Paris Olympics medal in kayak cross, but sister Noemie is still a shot - ABC News
description: Jessica Fox says she is "gutted" that she won't get a chance to win a third Paris Olympics medal, but that she was proud little sister Noemie still has a shot.
keywords: Jessica Fox, Noemie Fox, kayak, canoe, Paris Olympics, Paris Games, Paris 2024
